# KuratchiJS LLM Build Spec (Implementation-Grade)

Project: KuratchiJS
Package: @kuratchi/js
Primary docs page: /docs
Machine schema: /_assets/llms.json

This file is written for LLMs to scaffold real working apps.

## 1) What KuratchiJS is

KuratchiJS is a Cloudflare Workers-native framework.
It compiles HTML routes in src/routes into .kuratchi output used by Wrangler.

Key capabilities:
- File-based routing from HTML files
- Server actions using native FormData
- Durable Object RPC via .do.ts files
- Progressive enhancement with data-* attributes
- Config-driven ORM/Auth/DO integrations

## 2) Minimal Hello World (no ORM/auth)

### 2.1 Create folder structure

my-app/
- package.json
- tsconfig.json
- wrangler.jsonc
- kuratchi.config.ts
- src/routes/layout.html
- src/routes/page.html

### 2.2 package.json

{
  "name": "my-app",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "bun run --bun ../../packages/kuratchi-js/src/cli.ts dev",
    "build": "bun run --bun ../../packages/kuratchi-js/src/cli.ts build"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@kuratchi/js": "workspace:*"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "wrangler": "^4.65.0",
    "@cloudflare/workers-types": "^4.20260223.0"
  }
}

### 2.3 tsconfig.json

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ESNext",
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "strict": true,
    "types": ["./worker-configuration.d.ts"]
  },
  "include": ["src/**/*.ts", "kuratchi.config.ts"]
}

### 2.4 wrangler.jsonc

{
  "name": "my-app",
  "main": ".kuratchi/worker.js",
  "compatibility_date": "2026-03-06",
  "compatibility_flags": ["nodejs_compat"]
}

### 2.5 kuratchi.config.ts

import { defineConfig } from '@kuratchi/js';

export default defineConfig({});

### 2.6 src/routes/layout.html

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <title>My App</title>
</head>
<body>
  <header><a href="/">Home</a></header>
  <main><slot></slot></main>
</body>
</html>

### 2.7 src/routes/page.html

<h1>Hello KuratchiJS</h1>
<p>Running on Cloudflare Workers.</p>

### 2.8 Run

- bun install
- bun run build
- bun run dev

## 3) Route conventions

- src/routes/page.html -> /
- src/routes/about/page.html -> /about
- src/routes/blog/[slug]/page.html -> /blog/:slug
- src/routes/layout.html wraps all pages and must contain <slot></slot>

## 4) Basic dynamic server data

Put server code in top <script> block of a route file:

<script>
  const framework = 'KuratchiJS';
  const year = new Date().getUTCFullYear();
</script>

<h1>{framework}</h1>
<p>{year}</p>

## 5) Forms + actions (important)

Action function in route script:

<script>
  import { ActionError, redirect } from '@kuratchi/js';

  export async function createPost(formData: FormData) {
    const title = String(formData.get('title') || '').trim();
    if (!title) throw new ActionError('Title is required');
    redirect('/');
  }
</script>

<form action={createPost} method="POST">
  <input name="title" />
  <button type="submit">Create</button>
</form>

Rules:
- Exported function + action binding => server action
- Action input is FormData
- Use ActionError for user-facing validation errors
- Use redirect() for PRG flow

## 6) Durable Object RPC quickstart

- Create src/server/notes.do.ts
- Export functions (function mode preferred)
- Configure durableObjects in kuratchi.config.ts and wrangler.jsonc
- Import generated RPC functions from $durable-objects/notes

Example DO file:

export async function listNotes() {
  return [{ id: 1, title: 'Hello from DO' }];
}

Example route usage:

<script>
  import { listNotes } from '$durable-objects/notes';
  const notes = await listNotes();
</script>

for (const note of notes) {
  <p>{note.title}</p>
}

## 7) Progressive enhancement shortcuts

- data-action: call server action via fetch
- data-refresh: refresh partial blocks after action
- data-get: declarative navigation
- data-poll: poll RPC endpoint
- data-select-all / data-select-item: checkbox group sync

## 8) Compiler output and deploy

Build emits:
- .kuratchi/routes.js
- .kuratchi/worker.js
- .kuratchi/do/*.js (when using DO RPC)

Wrangler should always point to:
- main: .kuratchi/worker.js

## 9) LLM generation checklist (must satisfy)

When generating a Kuratchi app, ensure:
- src/routes/layout.html exists and contains <slot></slot>
- src/routes/page.html exists
- wrangler main is .kuratchi/worker.js
- package scripts include build/dev via kuratchi CLI
- bun run build succeeds

If user asks for "basic site", generate:
- layout.html with nav
- home page
- one extra route (/about)
- one action form example
